decision_rationale: no_per_pass_fps_evidence: the GRX contract requires a per-pass FPS >= 0.95x baseline measured_local comparison before any default enablement (GRX_PLAN GRX.4 exit criteria / GRX_CONTRACT G-GRX-5 single_scene_fps_ratio_min=0.95), and no full baseline or per-pass benchmark comparison for ssao_blur exists yet

patch_0016_writeback_is_scaffold: the MODE_SMART result writeback is dispatched into the real Godot deinterleaved ping-pong slice while the native Godot SSAO blur continuation still re-renders the full blur_passes x 4-slice ping-pong chain every frame as the backstop, so the candidate image cannot change (LDR visual gate max_abs=0/mean_abs=0) and the opt-in Rurix dispatch yields no measured net benefit yet

mode_smart_single_pass_single_slice_subset: math parity covers only the MODE_SMART single-blur-pass single-deinterleaved-slice edge-aware cross subset (math_parity_evidence.json status=pending_gpu_dispatch); MODE_WIDE, MODE_NON_SMART, the multi-pass ping-pong chain, the 4-slice deinterleaved loop, SSIL blur (RXGD_PASS_SSIL_BLUR), mirror-sampler border addressing, and rg8 unorm storage quantization are recorded gaps
does_not_imply: broader ssao_blur mode / multi-pass / multi-slice / SSIL coverage beyond the MODE_SMART single-pass single-slice subset
net-benefit Rurix dispatch (patch 0016 result writeback is a scaffold; the native Godot SSAO blur ping-pong chain re-renders every frame)
